badge.title: Badge
badge.description: Inline label to show state, counts or categories. Supports solid / subtle / outline variants, semantic colors, sizes, icon and dot mode.

variants.title: Variants
variants.desc: Three visual treatments: solid for emphasis, subtle by default and outline for discretion.

icon.title: With icon
icon.desc: Add an icon to the left of the label to reinforce meaning.

dot.title: Dot mode
dot.desc: Replaces the icon with a dot to indicate presence or availability state.

props.label: Badge text. Equivalent to the default slot for plain content.
props.variant: Visual style applied to the badge.
props.color: Semantic color applied to the badge.
props.gradient: Applies a subtle gradient (only with solid variant).
props.size: Badge size. Cascades from ModoProvider when omitted.
props.icon: Icon component rendered to the left of the label.
props.removable: Shows a close button that emits the remove event.
props.dot: Replaces the icon with a small colored dot.
props.removeAriaLabel: Overrides the aria-label of the remove button.

emits.remove: Emitted when the close button is clicked while removable is active.

slots.default: Custom badge content. Replaces the label prop when HTML or inner components are needed.

types.title: Badge
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Badge component props. Supports solid, outline and subtle variants with semantic colors, dot mode and removable close button.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name for the badge
a11y.focusNative: Non-interactive by default; removable badges use a focusable close button

banner.title: Banner
banner.description: Prominent notice to communicate state at page or section level. Supports semantic colors, variants, dismiss and sticky position.

colors.title: Colors
colors.desc: Four semantic colors with default icons.

variants.title: Variants
variants.desc: Three visual treatments: filled, subtle (default) and outline.

action.title: With action
action.desc: Right-side actions slot to anchor a CTA.

dismiss.title: Dismissable
dismiss.desc: Lets the user close the banner. Emits the dismiss event.

icon.title: With custom icon
icon.desc: Replace the icon with your own via the icon prop.

demo.infoDesc: Your account will sync in a few minutes.
demo.renewDesc: Renew to keep access to premium features.
demo.newDesc: v2 ships theming support and new components.

props.variant: Visual style applied to the banner.
props.title: Banner highlighted title.
props.description: Descriptive text. Replaced by the default slot when provided.
props.dismissable: Shows a close button that emits the dismiss event.
props.dismissAnimation: Animation used when closing the banner.
props.icon: Custom icon. Falls back to the default by color when omitted.
props.showIcon: Shows or hides the banner icon.
props.fullWidth: Removes the radius to anchor the banner edge-to-edge.

emits.dismiss: Emitted when the banner is closed while dismissable is active.

slots.default: Banner body content. Replaces the description prop when HTML is needed.

types.title: Banner
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Banner component props. Supports semantic colors, filled/subtle/outline variants, dismiss animations and sticky positioning.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbEnter: Activate the dismiss button when focused
a11y.ariaAlert: Uses role alert to announce the banner to screen readers
a11y.ariaLive: Announces banner content changes to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name when no visible title is present
a11y.focusNative: Banner content is in the normal tab order
a11y.focusDismiss: The dismiss button is focusable and keyboard-activatable

chip.title: Chip
chip.description: Overlay indicator (counter or dot) positioned over another element like an avatar or icon. Also works as an inline pill when there is no anchor.

counter.title: Counter over icon
counter.desc: Overlay counter on an icon. Use max to cap the value at 99+.

dot.title: Dot over avatar
dot.desc: Presence indicator with no content over an avatar.

variants.title: Variants
variants.desc: Three visual treatments for the overlay chip.

inline.title: Inline (no anchor)
inline.desc: When there is no default slot, the chip renders in flow as a pill.

props.content: Visible content (number or short string). Ignored when dot is active.
props.dot: Renders a small indicator with no content.
props.invisible: Hides the chip while keeping it in the tree.
props.showZero: Shows the chip even when content is 0 (hidden by default).
props.variant: Visual style applied to the chip.
props.color: Semantic color applied to the chip.
props.size: Chip size. Cascades from ModoProvider when omitted.
props.placement: Anchor corner where the overlay chip is positioned.
props.icon: Optional decorative icon (ignored in dot mode).
props.ariaLabel: Accessible name. Recommended for numeric or dot chips.

slots.default: Anchor for the overlay chip. If omitted, the chip renders inline as a pill.

types.title: Chip
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Chip component props. Includes ChipPlacement union type for absolute positioning over anchors.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name for the chip content
a11y.ariaHidden: Decorative chips can be hidden from screen readers
a11y.focusNative: Non-interactive by default; positioned chips do not capture focus

confirmDialog.title: ConfirmDialog
confirmDialog.description: Imperative confirmation dialog. Mount the ConfirmDialog host once near the root and call useConfirm().confirm(\{...\}) from any component to get a Promise(boolean).

playground.title: Continue with this action?
playground.description: Component preview with the current playground settings.

basic.title: Basic confirmation
basic.desc: Passing just a string gives you a minimal yes/no dialog.

danger.title: Destructive action
danger.desc: For deletions and irreversible operations, use color='danger' with an explicit confirmLabel.

async.title: Async confirm
async.desc: confirm() returns Promise(boolean): perfect for chaining await + API calls.

custom.title: Custom labels
custom.desc: Override confirmLabel and cancelLabel so the button reflects the exact action.

props.radius: Default radius for confirmations that don't specify one.

types.title: ConfirmDialog
types.desc: TypeScript interfaces for the confirm system. Includes ConfirmInput with ConfirmColor and the ConfirmDialog host props for imperative confirmation dialogs.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element inside the dialog
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element inside the dialog
a11y.kbEsc: Closes the dialog and resolves with false
a11y.ariaDialog: Exposed as a dialog for screen readers
a11y.ariaModal: Communicates that the dialog blocks the rest of the page
a11y.ariaLabelledBy: Links the dialog title for screen reader context
a11y.focusTrap: Focus is trapped inside the dialog while it is open
a11y.focusReturn: Focus returns to the trigger element when the dialog closes

drawer.title: Drawer
drawer.description: Panel that slides in from any side of the viewport. Great for filters, settings, details and mobile bottom-sheets. Supports focus trap and scroll lock.

right.title: Right side (default)
right.desc: The default placement. Great for secondary details without leaving the current view.

sides.title: Top and bottom-sheet
sides.desc: Top works well for notifications; bottom mimics mobile bottom-sheet behavior.

form.title: With content (form)
form.desc: The drawer adapts to content — useful for secondary forms and quick edits.
form.drawerDesc: Quick edit for the selected project.

props.side: Side the panel slides in from.
props.size: Size along the panel's main axis.
props.radius: Corner radius (the edge touching the viewport side is not rounded).
props.title: Accessible title — rendered in header when no slot is provided.
props.showClose: Show the close (X) button in the header.
props.color: Accent color applied to border and ring.
props.innerBorder: Subtle inner border inside the panel.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible label when there is no title.

slots.header: Replaces the header generated by title/description.
slots.footer: Actions at the bottom of the drawer.

types.title: Drawer
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Drawer component props. Includes DrawerSide, DrawerSize and DrawerOverlay union types for side, size and backdrop configuration.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element inside the drawer
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element inside the drawer
a11y.kbEsc: Close the drawer when closeOnEscape is enabled
a11y.ariaDialog: Exposed as a dialog for screen readers
a11y.ariaModal: Communicates that the drawer is a modal blocking the rest of the page
a11y.ariaLabelledBy: Links the drawer title to the dialog for screen reader context
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links the description to the dialog for additional context
a11y.focusTrap: Focus is trapped inside the drawer while it is open
a11y.focusReturn: Focus returns to the trigger element when the drawer closes

emptyState.title: EmptyState
emptyState.description: Empty state for lists, searches and data-less sections. Supports icon, description, actions and visual variants.

playground.title: No notifications
playground.description: When you receive something it will appear here.

action.title: With action
action.desc: Actions slot for adding a CTA below the description.
action.emptyDesc: Try a different search or clear the filters.

variants.title: Variants
variants.desc: Three surfaces: subtle (default), solid (with border) and outline (dashed border).

noResults.title: No results
noResults.desc: Typical pattern for a search with no matches.
noResults.emptyDesc: We couldn't find anything matching "invoices 2025". Try other terms.

error.title: Error state
error.desc: Combine variant outline with a CTA to retry the operation.
error.emptyDesc: Please retry in a few seconds.

props.title: Main heading of the empty state.
props.icon: Icon shown above the title. Defaults to InboxIcon.
props.size: Visual size of the empty state.
props.radius: Corner radius for solid or outline variants.

slots.default: Extra content rendered at the end of the empty state.

types.title: EmptyState
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the EmptyState component props. Includes EmptyStateSize and EmptyStateVariant union types for size and visual treatment.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.ariaRegion: Uses role region to identify the empty state as a navigable landmark
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name for the empty state region
a11y.focusNative: Actions inside the empty state are focusable; the container itself is not

loader.title: Loader
loader.description: Circular spinner for indicating indeterminate progress. Small, accessible, and inherits color by default so it integrates into buttons, banners or any container.

variants.title: Variants
variants.desc: normal shows only the arc; outline adds a subtle background track.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three sizes aligned with the rest of the system (14, 18 and 22 px).

colors.title: Custom colors
colors.desc: The color prop accepts any CSS value — useful for applying project semantic tokens.

inline.title: Inline with text
inline.desc: Combined with text, the spinner inherits the container's color by default.

props.variant: Visual variant. outline paints a subtle background track on the ring.
props.color: Stroke color. Accepts any valid CSS value.
props.label: Visually hidden accessible text for screen readers.

types.title: Loader
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Loader component props. Simple spinner with variant, size, color and accessible label.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.ariaStatus: Uses role status to announce loading state to screen readers
a11y.ariaLive: Announces loading state changes to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaLabel: Provides an accessible name via the label prop
a11y.focusNative: Non-interactive element; does not participate in the tab order

modal.title: Modal
modal.description: Centered modal dialog with focus trap, scroll lock, and FLIP-style animation from the triggering button. Supports sizes, header with title/description, and a footer slot.

playground.title: Confirm action
playground.body: Do you want to apply these changes?

basic.title: Basic
basic.desc: Triggered by a button with v-model. Footer with cancel/confirm actions.
basic.modalDesc: Do you want to apply these changes?
basic.body: The change is reversible from the history.

form.title: With form
form.desc: Useful for creating or editing entities without leaving the current context.
form.modalDesc: Create a new project to track your work.

locked.title: No overlay-close
locked.desc: For critical actions, force an explicit decision by disabling overlay and Escape.
locked.body: Only closes with the explicit button.

full.title: Fullscreen
full.desc: size='full' fills the entire viewport — ideal for immersive detail views.

scroll.title: Scrollable content
scroll.desc: The modal body can scroll internally without blocking the rest of the layout.
scroll.paragraph: Paragraph {i} with long text to force internal scroll.

props.title: Accessible title — rendered in header when no slot is provided.
props.showClose: Show the close (X) button in the corner.
props.color: Accent color applied to border and ring.
props.innerBorder: Renders a subtle inner border inside the main border.
props.origin: Origin element for the FLIP-style animation.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible label when there is no title.

slots.header: Replaces the header generated from title/description.
slots.footer: Actions at the bottom of the modal (cancel/confirm buttons).

types.title: Modal
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Modal component props. Includes ModalSize, ModalPosition and ModalOverlay union types for size, position and backdrop configuration.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element inside the modal
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element inside the modal
a11y.kbEsc: Close the modal when closeOnEscape is enabled
a11y.ariaDialog: Exposed as a dialog for screen readers
a11y.ariaModal: Communicates that the modal is blocking the rest of the page
a11y.ariaLabelledBy: Links the modal title to the dialog for screen reader context
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links the description to the dialog for additional context
a11y.focusTrap: Focus is trapped inside the modal while it is open
a11y.focusReturn: Focus returns to the trigger element when the modal closes

progressBar.title: ProgressBar
progressBar.description: Determinate or indeterminate linear progress bar. Supports label, numeric value, colors, sizes and striped animation.

label.title: With label and value
label.desc: Combine a label above and showValue to display the numeric percentage.

indeterminate.title: Indeterminate
indeterminate.desc: Omit the value prop to show the sliding animation.

colors.title: Colors
colors.desc: Five semantic colors to reflect the process state.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.desc: Three thicknesses: small, medium (default) and large.

striped.title: Striped
striped.desc: Enable animated stripes for in-progress operations.

props.value: Current value between min and max. Omit for indeterminate mode.
props.showValue: Shows the numeric percentage next to the bar.
props.label: Optional label rendered above the bar.
props.ariaLabel: Accessible label when the label prop is omitted.

types.title: ProgressBar
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the ProgressBar component props. Includes ProgressBarColor and ProgressBarSize union types with determinate and indeterminate modes.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.ariaProgress: Uses role progressbar for screen reader identification
a11y.ariaValueNow: Announces the current progress value to screen readers
a11y.ariaValueMin: Communicates the minimum value (0 by default)
a11y.ariaValueMax: Communicates the maximum value (100 by default)
a11y.focusNative: Non-interactive element; does not participate in the tab order

skeleton.title: Skeleton
skeleton.description: Animated placeholder that reserves content space while loading. Supports presets that replicate the exact footprint of real components to prevent layout shifts.

text.title: Multi-line text
text.desc: Use shape='text' with lines to simulate a paragraph. The last line is automatically shortened.

profile.title: Avatar + text
profile.desc: Typical composition for user lists: circle + two stacked lines.

card.title: Card placeholder
card.desc: Reserves the footprint of a card with image, title and description.

button.title: Button skeleton
button.desc: The button preset respects the height per size to match the real button.

props.shape: Visual preset that defines default height, width and radius. See the interface for all available presets.
props.size: Maps to the real component's size to maintain pixel-parity.
props.radius: Corner radius. circle / avatar always force full.
props.width: Explicit width. Accepts any CSS length.
props.fullWidth: Collapses width to 100% of the container.
props.withLabel: Renders a mini label bar above the shape.
props.lines: For shape="text": number of stacked lines.

types.title: Skeleton
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Skeleton component props. Extensive shape preset system that mirrors real component footprints to prevent layout shifts.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.ariaHidden: Hidden from screen readers to avoid announcing placeholder content
a11y.ariaBusy: Marks the parent region as busy while skeletons are visible
a11y.focusNative: Non-interactive element; does not participate in the tab order

toast.title: ToastContainer
toast.description: Singleton container where toasts fired by the useToast() composable appear. Mount it once near the root and push notifications from any component.

error.title: Error / danger
error.desc: For network errors, failed validation or any blocking state.

info.title: Info pinned
info.desc: Passing duration: 0 keeps the toast anchored until the user dismisses it or you call dismiss().

warning.title: Warning with action
warning.desc: Add an inline action button — useful for 'Undo', 'Review' or 'Retry'.

dismissAll.title: Dismiss all
dismissAll.desc: Push multiple toasts at once, then clear the entire queue with dismissAll().

props.max: Maximum number of visible toasts; older ones are evicted.
props.defaultDuration: Default duration in ms for toasts without a duration.
props.defaultVariant: Default variant applied to push() calls.
props.offset: Distance in px from the viewport edge.

types.title: ToastContainer
types.desc: TypeScript interfaces for the toast system. Includes ToastInput, ToastAction, ToastColor and ToastPlacement types for configuring ephemeral notifications.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus into the toast container when toasts are present
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.ariaStatus: Uses role status to announce toast messages to screen readers
a11y.ariaLive: Announces toast content changes to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaAtomic: Announces the entire toast content as a single unit
a11y.focusNative: Toasts are focusable for keyboard dismissal
a11y.focusDismiss: Each toast has a dismiss button that is focusable and keyboard-activatable

toastItem.title: Toast
toastItem.description: Ephemeral notification rendered by ToastContainer. Not instantiated directly: fired via the info / success / warning / danger helpers of the useToast() composable.

playground.title: Notification
playground.description: Preview with the current playground settings.

colors.title: Semantic colors
colors.desc: Four helpers cover the common states: informational, success, warning and error.

action.title: With action
action.desc: action adds an inline button — the classic 'Undo' pattern after a deletion.
action.toastDesc: We sent it to the trash.

icon.title: Custom icon
icon.desc: Pass any icon component (heroicons or other) to replace the default icon.

pinned.title: Pinned (no auto-close)
pinned.desc: duration: 0 keeps the toast until the user dismisses it or you call dismiss().

props.title: Main title of the toast.
props.icon: Custom leading icon. Defaults to one chosen by color.
props.duration: Auto-close in ms. Use 0 to keep the toast pinned.
props.pauseOnHover: Pauses the timer while the cursor is over the toast.
props.id: Stable ID. If reused, the toast updates in-place.
props.radius: Radius override; cascades from provider by default.
props.placement: Position override for this specific toast.

types.title: ToastInput
types.desc: TypeScript interface for a toast item. Defines the shape passed to useToast().push() including color, variant, duration, action and placement.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the next focusable element inside the toast
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element inside the toast
a11y.kbEsc: Dismiss the toast when dismissable is enabled
a11y.ariaStatus: Uses role status to announce the toast message
a11y.ariaLive: Announces toast content changes to assistive technologies
a11y.ariaAtomic: Announces the entire toast content as a single unit
a11y.focusNative: Toast container is focusable for keyboard dismissal
a11y.focusDismiss: The dismiss button is focusable and keyboard-activatable

tooltip.title: Tooltip
tooltip.description: Contextual hint shown on hover or focus over an element. Supports multiple placements, semantic colors, configurable delays and rich content.

placements.title: Placements
placements.desc: Four main placements. Each variant also accepts -start and -end to align with a trigger edge.

rich.title: Rich content
rich.desc: Use the content slot to include markup, icons or keyboard shortcuts inside the panel.
rich.heading: Keyboard shortcut
rich.shortcut: Press Ctrl+K to open the search.

icon.title: Over icon-only button
icon.desc: Essential on icon-only buttons to describe the action to screen reader users.

delays.title: Delays
delays.desc: Adjust openDelay to avoid intrusive tooltips when moving the cursor between buttons.

props.content: Tooltip text. Use the content slot for markup.
props.offset: Distance in px between trigger and panel.
props.arrow: Shows an arrow pointing to the trigger.
props.wrapperClass: Extra classes for the trigger wrapper.

emits.updateOpen: Change in open/closed state of the tooltip.

slots.default: Trigger element over which the tooltip is shown.
slots.content: Custom panel content. Replaces the content prop.

types.title: Tooltip
types.desc: TypeScript interface for the Tooltip component props. Includes TooltipPlacement and TooltipTrigger union types for positioning and interaction control.

a11y.kbTab: Move focus to the trigger; tooltip opens on focus
a11y.kbShiftTab: Move focus to the previous focusable element
a11y.kbEsc: Close the tooltip when it is open
a11y.ariaTooltip: The panel uses role tooltip for screen reader identification
a11y.ariaDescribedBy: Links the tooltip text to the trigger element for screen reader context
a11y.focusNative: The trigger element remains focusable; the tooltip is announced on focus
a11y.focusKeyboard: Escape closes the tooltip; focus movement keeps it open
